tldr: Replace Fastly delivery with Netlify when the workload is a Git-managed site, porting VCL to redirects/headers/edge functions and Compute@Edge to Netlify Edge Functions. Expect significant rework if your Fastly setup is heavy on VCL or proxies arbitrary origins.
why_switch: Git-based deploys with previews and rollbacks
Edge Functions and Functions in one platform
Generous free tier for static sites
Lower operational overhead than VCL services
export_method: Use the Fastly API or CLI to export each service version as JSON, including backends, request/response conditions, headers, and any custom VCL. Download Compute@Edge source from your repository. Export ACLs and dictionaries via API. Document logging endpoints and TLS configuration. Pull recent access logs to baseline traffic patterns, top URLs, and cache-hit ratios. Note: managed TLS private keys may not be exportable from Fastly; plan to re-issue at cutover. Capture WAF rules and rate-limiting policies separately so they can be reproduced in the destination platform.
import_method: Create a Netlify site connected to your Git repository (or upload a build artifact). Translate Fastly VCL behavior into Netlifys netlify.toml redirects, rewrites, and headers; cases that VCL handled with logic typically become Netlify Edge Functions. Port Compute@Edge code to Edge Functions, noting that the runtime is Deno-based with web standard APIs and quotas. Configure custom domains and let Netlify provision TLS, or upload your own. Set up Functions for back-end logic that previously lived in your origin or Compute@Edge. Update DNS to delegate to Netlify DNS or add the Netlify load-balancer records. Use the Netlify CLI to test locally and stage previews before promoting to production.
effort: 3-8 weeks depending on VCL complexity and origin model
gotchas: Fastly annual commits and minimums must be reviewed before terminating; budget notice time
Netlify is Git-deploy oriented; pure proxy CDN use cases fit less naturally
VCL has no direct mapping; treat the migration as a redesign of edge logic
Compute@Edge (WASI/Rust/Go) and Edge Functions (Deno) are very different runtimes
WAF coverage may need an external provider if your security posture requires Next-Gen WAF features
Bandwidth-heavy or media-heavy workloads may be more expensive on Netlify; model the cost

email.subject: Migrating from Fastly to Netlify over the next 14 days
email.body:
Hello Fastly team,

Quick courtesy note: we plan to move our delivery from Fastly to Netlify over the next 14 days. The driver is consolidation of Git-based deploys, CDN, and edge functions onto one platform; this is a fit-of-tooling decision rather than a complaint about Fastly.

Plan: days 1-3 export service configs, VCL, Compute@Edge code, ACLs, dictionaries, and logging configs; days 4-9 build the Netlify site with redirects/headers and port edge logic to Edge Functions; days 10-12 cut DNS with low TTLs and keep Fastly warm as rollback; days 13-14 drain traffic and start contract wind-down.

Could you confirm our remaining commit, the notice period for non-renewal, and the recommended steps to deactivate services and TLS without disrupting anything in flight. Guidance on exporting any reusable TLS material would also help.

Thanks for the partnership.

Your customer
